prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for ferrocene (21 atoms: Fe + two η⁵-cyclopentadienyl rings) using the GFN2-xTB semi-empirical tight-binding method (Bannwarth, Ehlert & Grimme, J. Chem. Theory Comput. 2019, 15, 1652–1671), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Partial charges were obtained via Mulliken population analysis. The N−1 (cation) state used charge +1, multiplicity 2; the N+1 (anion) state used charge −1, multiplicity 2.

The headline result is the largest nucleophilic Fukui index f⁻ = 0.25938191, located on the iron centre (atom 11). Iron also carries the largest electrophilic index f⁺ = 0.22885456 and a negative dual descriptor (f⁺ − f⁻ = −0.030527), consistent with Fe acting as a nucleophilic / Lewis-basic site. Both Cp rings are perfectly equivalent by symmetry; all ring carbons show f⁺ ≈ 0.0136 and f⁻ ≈ 0.0122, and ring hydrogens show f⁺ ≈ 0.0635 and f⁻ ≈ 0.0619.

Integrity: status ok, trustworthy true. Both f⁺ and f⁻ sum to exactly 1.0000 (charge-conservation checks passed). No engine warnings were reported.

Caveat: GFN2-xTB Mulliken-based Fukui indices are suitable for qualitative reactivity trends only; a DFT-level treatment would be needed for quantitative values.
